objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: breakout_donchian_adx
archetype: breakout_donchian_adx
family: breakout
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- donchian(period=35)
- adx(period=25)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close > donchian.upper and adx.adx > 35
- short: close < donchian.lower and adx.adx > 35
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, donchian.middle) or adx.adx < 15
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 1.75
tp_atr_mult: 3.5
description: ATR stop-loss (1.75x) and take-profit (3.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: A Bollinger breakout with an ADX trend filter reduces false signals in ranging markets.
Combining the strength of a Bollinger band breakout with an ADX trend filter reduces false signals in ranging markets and takes advantage of confirmed trends.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy seeks to capture trend reversals using Bollinger Bands and RSI while filtering out false signals with ADX. This is achieved by combining these indicators in a way that minimizes whipsaws and maximizes directional moves.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy uses Bollinger Bands, RSI and ADX to identify trend reversals. The stop loss is set at 1.75 times the ATR while take profit is set at 3.5 times the ATR. It trades every time the price closes above or below the Donchian bands and when the ADX indicator is over 25. If this strategy doesn't perform well, I would first investigate if there are any issues with the indicators themselves like wrong inputs, incorrect parameters etc. Then, if that's not the case, I might want to try changing the stop loss or take profit values and see how it affects performance. Another approach could be trying a different combination of Bollinger Bands, RSI and ADX.
